title: boechera crandallii subsp. crandallii
intro: Boechera crandallii subsp. crandallii, also known as Crandall's rockcress, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States. It is a member of the mustard family and has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, due to its high content of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, and it has also been used in capsule or powder form to support overall health and well-being.
